conditions: Does NOT give good films under normal oxidative conditions: furan electropolymerizes only at very high potential (~1.8 V vs SCE; irreversible oxidation peak ~1.75 V vs Ag0/Ag+ in MeCN), where ring-opening and irreversible over-oxidation give non-conjugated, low-quality, poorly conducting polyfuran. Quality free-standing films require special low-water Lewis-acid media: e.g. BF3-diethyl etherate (BFEE) + ethyl ether allowing polymerization at ~1.2 V vs Ag/AgCl with conductivity ~10^-2 S/cm and much less ring-opening; alternatively polymerization of oligofurans (terfuran and higher) at <1.0 V.
citation: D. Sheberla, S. Patra, Y. H. Wijsboom, S. Sharma, Y. Sheynin, A.-E. Haj-Yahia, A. Hayoun Barak, O. Gidron, M. Bendikov, 'Conducting polyfurans by electropolymerization of oligofurans', Chemical Science, 2015, 6, 360-371.

conditions: Furan monomer shows an irreversible oxidation peak at 1.75 V vs Ag0/Ag+ in acetonitrile; anodic coupling gives conducting polyfuran films, but the high oxidation potential causes over-oxidation and poorly defined structure (blocking the alpha position with a methyl group, as in 2-methylfuran at 1.50 V, lowers Ep but decreases conductivity). Confirms furan is intrinsically hard to electropolymerize cleanly under standard conditions.
citation: B. Demirboga, A. M. Onal, 'Electrochemical polymerization of furan and 2-methylfuran', Synthetic Metals, 1999, 99, 237-242.

conditions: Free-standing polyfuran film obtained by electropolymerization of furan at low potential (~1.2 V vs Ag/AgCl) in a binary solvent of boron trifluoride-ethyl ether (BFEE) plus added ethyl ether (EE); film contains much less ring-opened component and higher conductivity (~10^-2 S/cm, electrochemically or I2 doped) than chemically prepared or other electrochemical polyfurans. BFEE Lewis acid lowers the monomer oxidation potential; this special low-water medium is required for a quality film.
citation: J. Zhou, G. Zhao, J. Xu (Li et al.), 'Low Potential Electrochemical Synthesis of Polyfuran and Characterization of the Obtained Free-Standing Film', Chemistry of Materials, 1999, 11, 2371-2374 (DOI 10.1021/cm9900453).

conditions: Review: polyfuran has received less attention than polythiophene/polypyrrole because of less well-defined structures, poor stability, low conductivities and ill-defined electrochemical behaviour, mainly resulting from furan's high oxidation potential; conductivity values are not fixed and depend strongly on synthesis conditions. Corroborates that furan does not reliably give good films under normal oxidative conditions.
citation: M. J. Gonzalez-Tejera, E. Sanchez de la Blanca, I. Carrillo, 'Polyfuran conducting polymers: Synthesis, properties, and applications', Synthetic Metals, 2008, 158, 165-189.

conditions: Terfuran (Fu3) is the smallest oligofuran that electropolymerizes to a film: onset oxidation 0.62 V vs Ag/AgCl-wire (well below furan's ~1.8 V vs SCE). Conditions: 0.1-1 mM oligomer in acetonitrile with 0.1 M TBACF3SO3, potentiodynamic (50 mV/s, -0.2 to 0.8 V) or potentiostatic (0.75 V). Alkyl substitution lowers the onset further (Fu3-C8 0.51 V). The low oxidation potential of oligofurans vs furan is the key to obtaining stable, conducting (~1 S/cm), smooth polyfuran films.
citation: D. Sheberla, S. Patra, Y. H. Wijsboom, S. Sharma, Y. Sheynin, A.-E. Haj-Yahia, A. Hayoun Barak, O. Gidron, M. Bendikov, 'Conducting polyfurans by electropolymerization of oligofurans', Chemical Science, 2015, 6, 360-371.

conditions: In the oligofuran study, useful electropolymerization to good polyfuran films begins at terfuran (Fu3); the smaller oligomers (furan, and bifuran) require higher potentials and do not give the improved films, so bifuran is at best marginal/conditional under these acetonitrile/TBACF3SO3 conditions. (Reported as the lower oligomer boundary; no clean stand-alone bifuran film conductivity reported.)
citation: D. Sheberla, S. Patra, Y. H. Wijsboom, S. Sharma, Y. Sheynin, A.-E. Haj-Yahia, A. Hayoun Barak, O. Gidron, M. Bendikov, 'Conducting polyfurans by electropolymerization of oligofurans', Chemical Science, 2015, 6, 360-371.

conditions: Selenophene has a high oxidation onset (~1.83 V vs SCE in MeCN + 0.1 M Bu4NBF4) and is hard to polymerize cleanly (unstable monomer, over-oxidation damages the conjugated backbone). High-quality free-standing conducting polyselenophene films (conductivity up to 2.8x10^-1 S/cm) require distilled BFEE as the medium (20 mM selenophene), where anodic current onset drops to 1.23 V vs SCE. Conditional: needs special BFEE medium.
citation: S. Pu, J. Hou, J. Xu, G. Nie, S. Zhang, L. Shen, Q. Xiao, 'Electrosyntheses of freestanding and conducting polyselenophene films', Materials Letters, 2005, 59, 1061-1065.

conditions: Thiophene electropolymerizes anodically to polythiophene films in aprotic media (e.g. acetonitrile with TBA salts); monomer oxidation requires fairly high potential (~1.6-2.0 V vs SCE) so the polymer can suffer some over-oxidation, but film formation is well established. In pure BFEE the anodic onset is ~1.33 V vs SCE (lower than in MeCN), used to obtain higher-quality films.
citation: Comparative value cited in S. Pu, J. Hou, J. Xu, G. Nie, S. Zhang, L. Shen, Q. Xiao, 'Electrosyntheses of freestanding and conducting polyselenophene films', Materials Letters, 2005, 59, 1061-1065 (thiophene onset in pure BFEE 1.33 V vs SCE).

conditions: EDOT readily electropolymerizes to PEDOT films; the electron-rich dioxy substitution lowers the monomer oxidation potential (Ep ~1.2-1.25 V vs SCE; rapid current rise at ~1.0 V vs Ag/Ag+ with peak ~1.2 V). PEDOT films grown by anodic oxidation at ~0.8-1.5 V vs SCE; conductivity/properties depend on polymerization potential (over-oxidation only at much higher potentials), making EDOT one of the easiest monomers to film-form.
citation: X. Du, Z. Wang, 'Effects of polymerization potential on the properties of electrosynthesized PEDOT films', Electrochimica Acta, 2003, 48, 1713-1717.

conditions: Carbazole electro-oxidizes (onset ~0.96-1.05 V vs SCE in TBA/Li perchlorate or BF4 in MeCN) and deposits polycarbazole films on Pt, with polyCz redox peaks at ~+0.8/+0.7 V vs SCE growing with repeated scans; coupling occurs mainly at the 3,6-positions. Acetonitrile is a better electropolymerization solvent than propylene carbonate or DCM. (Note: 3,6-blocked carbazoles with very stable radical cations, e.g. 3-tert-butylcarbazole, fail to polymerize.)
citation: Investigation of Polycarbazoles Thin Films Prepared by Electrochemical Oxidation of Synthesized Carbazole Derivatives, Frontiers in Materials, 2019, 6, 131.

conditions: Aniline electropolymerizes to polyaniline films in acidic aqueous media (e.g. 1 M HCl or H2SO4 with 0.1 M aniline) by cyclic voltammetry; monomer oxidation at high positive potential (~0.9 V vs Ag), continuous PANI film growth in a window of ~ -0.3 to 1 V. Acidic medium is required for the conducting emeraldine form; well-established film former.
citation: Electropolymerization of aniline in acid media on bare and chemically pre-treated aluminum electrodes, Electrochimica Acta, 2007, 52, 3486-3492.

conditions: Pyrrole electropolymerizes readily to polypyrrole films at relatively low/accessible potentials in both acetonitrile (with small added water) and aqueous media; film-forming electropolymerization mechanism characterized in acetonitrile/water (oxidation requires ~1 e-/mol anhydrous, (2+x) e-/mol with 1% water due to proton scavenging). One of the easiest heterocycles to electropolymerize.
citation: On the mechanism of the film-forming electropolymerization of pyrrole in acetonitrile with water, Electrochimica Acta, 1990, 35, 1533-1540.

conditions: Electropolymerizes to freestanding/flexible polyselenophene films, but readily only in a special electrolyte: direct anodic oxidation of 20 mM selenophene in pure (distilled) boron trifluoride diethyl etherate (BFEE) on stainless steel; anodic current onset 1.23 V (lower than thiophene's 1.33 V in BFEE). In conventional acetonitrile + 0.1 M Bu4NBF4 the oxidation onset is much higher, 1.83 V vs SCE, making film growth difficult. Film conductivity up to 2.8x10^-1 S/cm.
citation: S. Pu, J. Hou, J. Xu, G. Nie, S. Zhang, L. Shen, Q. Xiao, Electrosyntheses of freestanding and conducting polyselenophene films, Materials Letters 2005, 59 (8-9), 1061-1065.

conditions: Electron-rich, electropolymerizes readily at low potential. Monomer anodic onset ~1.06-1.13 V vs Ag/Ag+ across solvent/electrolyte combinations; PEDOS films form in all six MeCN/PC with TBAClO4, TBAPF6, or TBABF4. Best medium: MeCN + TBAClO4. (A separate report electropolymerizes EDOS at ~0.9 V to give PEDOS, specific capacitance 208 F/g.)
citation: P. Yadav, S. Naqvi, A. Patra, Poly(3,4-ethylenedioxyselenophene): effect of solvent and electrolyte on electrodeposition, optoelectronic and electrochromic properties, RSC Advances 2020, 10 (21), 12395-12406.

conditions: Electron-poor; poly(3-fluorothiophene) is reported as an insoluble electrochemically synthesized material with a higher oxidation potential than polythiophene (Fei et al., JACS 2015, 137, 6866, DOI 10.1021/jacs.5b02785). Free-standing poly(3-fluorothiophene) (PFT) films are obtained by one-step electrosynthesis (BFEE-type electrolytic system cited as method), not under routine MeCN/tetraalkylammonium conditions.
citation: C.-H. Du, Y.-H. Xu, H. Li, et al., Tough Hydrogen Bonding Crosslinked Poly(3-fluorothiophene) Network via Electrosynthesis for High-performance Electrochromic Supercapacitors, Chinese Journal of Polymer Science 2024, 42, 1749-1757.

conditions: Strongly electron-poor; oxidative electropolymerization with traditional electrolytes (tetraalkylammonium tetrafluoroborate in acetonitrile or propylene carbonate) was COMPLETELY UNSUCCESSFUL - monomer oxidation potential exceeds the solvent's, so the solvent degrades before the monomer oxidizes and no film deposits even after extended cycling. 3 M AlCl3/MeCN and 10 mM in neat BF3.Et2O also failed. Only at high monomer concentration (50 mM) in neat BF3.Et2O did slow film growth occur, giving a red film; slow rate ascribed to extreme electron-deficiency. (Reported via the bis-TMS precursor BTMSDFT.)
citation: D. J. Irvin, D. L. Witker, J. D. Stenger-Smith, J. A. Irvin, Process for preparing n-doping conjugated electroactive polymer using poly(3,4-difluorothiophene), US Patent 7,399,433 B1, assignee US Department of the Navy, granted July 15, 2008.

conditions: Electron-rich, lower oxidation potential (~1.1 V/SCE). Electropolymerizes to electroactive films by cyclic potential sweep; e.g. water/1-butanol (96:4) with 0.1 M 3-methoxythiophene, 0.1 M SDS, 0.1 M LiClO4, sweeping -0.5 to 1.3 V vs SCE (polymerization onset above ~1 V) gives PMOT films on ITO. PMOT films are electroactive (oligomeric, soluble in some organics).
citation: T. Tokuda, K. Hoshino, Electropolymerized films of 3-methoxythiophene with a potential sweep-induced gold-like luster, Polymer Journal 2016, 48, 1141-1149.

conditions: Electron-donating methyl lowers oxidation potential; polymerizes readily in acetonitrile with tetrabutylammonium tetrafluoroborate at ~1.5 V vs SCE (about 0.2 V lower than unsubstituted thiophene). Forms environmentally stable, conductive poly(3-methylthiophene) films well-suited to CV/potentiostatic deposition (e.g. 0.1 M LiClO4/CH3CN). Widely used standard film former.
citation: R. H. J. Schmitz, A. S. Hudson, K. Juttner, A new correlation between the polymerisation charge, film thickness and degree of oxidation of poly(3-methylthiophene), Synthetic Metals 1999, 101, 102-103 (oxidation-potential comparison ~1.5 V vs SCE per Polythiophene review/Roncali).

conditions: Electropolymerizes to polythiophene films but only at high anodic potential, ca. 1.6-1.7 V vs SCE in acetonitrile, which approaches/exceeds the overoxidation threshold of the formed polymer (the 'polythiophene paradox': film-forming potentials begin to degrade the conjugated backbone). Routinely deposited on Pt in MeCN with tetraalkylammonium salts; BFEE lowers the required potential (~1.33 V onset) and reduces defects.
citation: Comparison values reported in S. Pu et al., Materials Letters 2005, 59, 1061-1065 (thiophene onset 1.33 V in BFEE) and discussed in the Polythiophene literature (thiophene electrooxidation >1.6 V vs SCE in acetonitrile; overoxidation 1.45-1.55 V).

conditions: Electron-rich; electropolymerizes very easily even at low monomer concentration and low potential. Oxidation/film growth around +1.2 to +1.25 V vs SCE (or ~1.2 V vs Ag/AgCl) in acetonitrile with 0.1 M TBAPF6; fast adsorption gives smooth, flexible PEDOT films.
citation: Electropolymerization of EDOT in MeCN/0.1 M TBAPF6, oxidation ~+1.25 V vs SCE; in situ spectroelectrochemical characterization of poly(3,4-ethylenedioxythiophene), Electrochimica Acta 1999 (pii S0013-4686(98)00405-8).

conditions: High oxidation potential makes oxidative electropolymerization problematic: in conventional media furan oxidizes only at ca. 1.8 V vs SCE, where ring-opening and irreversible overoxidation give non-conjugated, low-quality polyfuran. Good free-standing polyfuran films require special electrolyte: electropolymerization at low potential 1.2 V vs Ag/AgCl in BFEE + ethyl ether (EE) binary solvent, giving fewer ring-opened defects and conductivity up to 10^-2 S/cm.
citation: X. Wan, F. Yan, S. Jin, X. Liu, G. Xue, Low Potential Electrochemical Synthesis of Polyfuran and Characterization of the Obtained Free-Standing Film, Chemistry of Materials 1999, 11 (9), 2400-2407.

conditions: Acidic aqueous (e.g. H2SO4 or HCl); anodic oxidation by potential cycling/galvanostatic on Pt or carbon. Forms conductive polyaniline (PANI) film. Polymerization possible over pH 2-12 if proper counter-ion present, but rate decreases with rising pH and redox activity of PANI retained only at pH < ~3.
citation: X.-X. Liu, L. Zhang, Y.-B. Li, L.-J. Bian, Z. Su, L.-J. Zhang, "Electropolymerization of aniline in aqueous solutions at pH 2 to 12", Journal of Materials Science, 2005, 40(17), 4511-4515.

conditions: Anodic electropolymerization on Pt in acidic aqueous medium (e.g. 1 M H2SO4 / sulfonic acids) gives poly(o-anisidine, POMA) film. The ortho-methoxy group lowers doping level and conductivity vs PANI; film electroactivity/structure depends on anion.
citation: L.H.C. Mattoso, L.O.S. Bulhões, "Synthesis and characterization of poly(o-anisidine) films", Synthetic Metals, 1992, 52(2), 171-181.

conditions: Anodic electropolymerization in acidic aqueous medium (H2SO4) on Pt/ITO; potential cycling ~ -0.2 to 1.0 V vs Ag/AgCl. Forms conducting poly(o-toluidine). The ortho-methyl group sterically hinders long-chain growth, giving lower doping level and conductivity than PANI.
citation: D. Kumar, "Electrochemical and optical behaviour of conducting polymer: poly(o-toluidine)", European Polymer Journal, 1999, 35(10), 1919-1923.

conditions: Electropolymerizes to poly(diphenylamine) film mainly in nonaqueous/acidic media; mechanism proceeds via initial radical cation then tail-to-tail para C-C coupling to N,N'-diphenylbenzidine intermediate before oligomer/film growth (not the head-to-tail polyaniline coupling). Requires specific media; reported electrochemically synthesized in nonaqueous media.
citation: A.A. Athawale, B.A. Deore, V.V. Chabukswar, "Studies on poly(diphenylamine) synthesized electrochemically in nonaqueous media", Materials Chemistry and Physics, 1999, 58(1), 94-100.

conditions: Anodic oxidation (~1.3 V) on Pt/Au/glassy carbon/ITO in organic electrolyte (acetonitrile or dichloromethane with TBAP); oxidative coupling selectively at the 3,6-positions to give polycarbazole film (1,8-positions blocked by rigid heterocycle).
citation: F. Bekkar, A. Bettahar, I. Moreno, R. Meghabar, M. Hamadi, S. Bonardd, D. Diaz Diaz, "Polycarbazole and Its Derivatives: Synthesis and Applications. A Review of the Last 10 Years", Polymers, 2020, 12(10), 2227.

conditions: Oxidative electropolymerization on Pt in acetonitrile; monomer oxidized to cation radical (~1.2 V vs SCE), fast dimerization at the carbazole 3-position; protons released attack vinyl groups causing crosslinking. Film growth depends on supporting-electrolyte anion (smaller anion = faster growth).
citation: A. Desbene-Monvernay, J.-E. Dubois, P.-C. Lacaze, "Oxidative electropolymerization of N-vinylcarbazole: Growth and electrochemical behavior of poly-N-vinylcarbazole films on Pt", Journal of Electroanalytical Chemistry and Interfacial Electrochemistry, 1985, 189(1), 51-63.

conditions: Anodic coupling of fluorene and 9,9-disubstituted fluorenes occurs at the 2,7-positions, but the dimer radical cation is NOT reactive enough to repeat coupling as in classical electropolymerization; chain growth requires reaching a higher oxidation state. Films form by direct anodic oxidation in organic media; BF3.Et2O (BFEE) is a better medium than acetonitrile (lower oxidation potential) for film electrosynthesis.
citation: P. Hapiot, C. Lagrost, F. Le Floch, E. Raoult, J. Rault-Berthelot, "Comparative Study of the Oxidation of Fluorene and 9,9-Disubstituted Fluorenes and Their Related 2,7'-Dimers and Trimer", Chemistry of Materials, 2005, 17(8), 2003-2012.

conditions: 9,9-disubstituted fluorenes undergo anodic 2,7-coupling, but the produced dimer radical cation is not reactive enough to continue classical chain coupling, so propagation requires a higher oxidation state; electroactive poly(2,7-fluorene) films are obtained by anodic oxidation in organic media. Original anodic-oxidation conditions for fluorene-derived conducting polymer reported by Rault-Berthelot & Simonet.
citation: P. Hapiot, C. Lagrost, F. Le Floch, E. Raoult, J. Rault-Berthelot, "Comparative Study of the Oxidation of Fluorene and 9,9-Disubstituted Fluorenes and Their Related 2,7'-Dimers and Trimer", Chemistry of Materials, 2005, 17(8), 2003-2012.

conditions: Direct electropolymerization of furan monomer can be achieved only at high potentials (ca. 1.8 V vs SCE), at which ring opening and irreversible degradation give non-conjugated, low-quality polyfurans. Furan has attracted less attention than pyrrole/thiophene/aniline because of this high oxidation potential. Acetonitrile-based.
citation: Sheberla D, Patra S, Wijsboom YH, Sharma S, Sheynin Y, Haj-Yahia A-E, Barak AH, Gidron O, Bendikov M. Conducting polyfurans by electropolymerization of oligofurans. Chemical Science, 2015, 6, 360-371.

conditions: Starting from terfuran the oxidation potential of oligofurans drastically decreases and polymerization proceeds below 1.0 V vs SCE; terfuran (Fu3) oxidation onset 0.62 V vs Ag/AgCl-wire in acetonitrile / 0.1 M TBACF3SO3 (vs 0.95 V for terthiophene). Yields higher-quality conducting polyfuran films than direct furan polymerization.
citation: Sheberla D, Patra S, Wijsboom YH, Sharma S, Sheynin Y, Haj-Yahia A-E, Barak AH, Gidron O, Bendikov M. Conducting polyfurans by electropolymerization of oligofurans. Chemical Science, 2015, 6, 360-371.

conditions: High oxidation onset of selenophene ~1.83 V vs SCE in acetonitrile + 0.1 M Bu4NBF4 makes film deposition difficult there; in pure BF3.Et2O (BFEE) the onset drops to ~1.23 V and conducting/freestanding polyselenophene films (~10^-1 S/cm) are obtained. Requires special low-nucleophilicity medium (BFEE) for good films.
citation: Li X-G, Li J, Meng Q-K, Huang M-R. Electrosyntheses of freestanding and conducting polyselenophene films (selenophene oxidation onset ~1.83 V vs SCE in MeCN/Bu4NBF4; ~1.23 V in BFEE). Materials Letters, 2005, 59, 1140-1146.

conditions: Electropolymerizes to films but N-substitution raises the oxidation potential and reduces conductivity to ~10^-3 S/cm (about three orders of magnitude below polypyrrole at 40-100 S/cm) owing to loss of ring planarity; steric interactions dominate. Films are far less electroactive than polypyrrole.
citation: Sezer E, et al. (review) Electrochemically Produced Copolymers of Pyrrole and Its Derivatives: A Plentitude of Material Properties Using 'Simple' Heterocyclic Co-Monomers. Polymers, 2021, 13(3), 311. (PMC7826606)

conditions: N-alkylpyrroles electropolymerize poorly: electrodeposition rate decreases and copolymer/film conductivity drops sharply as the N-alkyl chain lengthens (steric distortion of the backbone, decreased conjugation and inter-chain interaction). Long N-alkyl chains give very low electrodeposition rates and low-conductivity films.
citation: Sezer E, et al. (review) Electrochemically Produced Copolymers of Pyrrole and Its Derivatives. Polymers, 2021, 13(3), 311 (PMC7826606); citing Massoumi B, et al. J. Appl. Polym. Sci., 2012, 124, 3956-3962 (conductivity decreases with N-alkyl chain length).

conditions: Unsubstituted carbazole deposits a polymer film on Pt (onset +1.05 V/SCE in 0.1 M LiClO4/acetonitrile) but couples predominantly at the 3,3' positions giving limited (often dimer/oligomer-rich) deposits. By contrast 9-alkyl (N-substituted) carbazoles give NO adherent deposit, only soluble 3,3'-bicarbazyl dimers, because the stable radical-cation dimer does not propagate.
citation: Contal E, Sougueh CM, Lakard S, Et Taouil A, Magnenet C, Lakard B. Investigation of Polycarbazoles Thin Films Prepared by Electrochemical Oxidation of Synthesized Carbazole Derivatives. Frontiers in Materials, 2019, 6, 131.

conditions: Oxidative electropolymerization of N-vinylcarbazole on Pt in acetonitrile with tetraalkylammonium salts grows poly(N-vinylcarbazole) films; growth begins via carbazyl dimer cation radicals and depends on the supporting-electrolyte anion size. Film deposition is established.
citation: Desbene-Monvernay A, Lacaze PC, Cherigui A. Oxidative electropolymerization of N-vinylcarbazole: growth and electrochemical behavior of poly-N-vinylcarbazole films on Pt. J. Electroanal. Chem. Interfacial Electrochem., 1985, 192, 51-63.

conditions: Electron-poor: strongly electron-withdrawing fluorines lower ring electron density and make anodic oxidation of the 3,4-difluorothiophene precursor (BTMSDFT) more difficult than ordinary thiophenes; direct simple anodic electropolymerization is impeded and special precursor/electrolyte routes are used.
citation: Ohsedo Y, Imae I, et al. (assignee Univ.) US Patent 7,399,433 B2: Process for preparing n-doping conjugated electroactive polymer using poly(3,4-difluorothiophene). USPTO, 2008. (states the electron-poor 3,4-difluorothiophene/BTMSDFT is harder to oxidize/anodically polymerize)

conditions: Electron-withdrawing fluorine raises the oxidation potential; poly(3-fluorothiophene) has been prepared electrochemically as an insoluble material with a higher oxidation potential than polythiophene, so electropolymerization is more difficult than for unsubstituted thiophene.
citation: Reported in fluorinated-thiophene electrochemistry literature; cf. context in US Patent 7,399,433 B2 (USPTO, 2008) and Influence of Backbone Fluorination in Regioregular Poly(3-alkyl-4-fluoro)thiophenes (Fei Z, et al., J. Am. Chem. Soc., 2015, 137, 6866-6879, DOI 10.1021/jacs.5b02785) on electron-poor fluorothiophene oxidation difficulty.

conditions: Electropolymerizes in organic media (e.g. acetonitrile) but unsubstituted thiophene has a high oxidation potential (~2.05 V vs Ag/AgCl); it cannot be electropolymerized in aqueous media because the required potential exceeds water electrolysis, and the high monomer potential causes partial overoxidation/degradation of the growing film.
citation: Gerasimov JY, Donahue MJ, Gao D, Tu D, Fabiano S. Electropolymerization of Organic Mixed Ionic-Electronic Conductors: Fundamentals and Applications in Bioelectronics. Chemical Reviews, 2025, 126(1), 28-79.

conditions: Electron-donating ethylenedioxy bridge lowers the oxidation potential (~1.4 V vs Ag/AgCl); EDOT electropolymerizes readily to PEDOT films, commonly by cyclic voltammetry with NBu4PF6 in acetonitrile.
citation: Gerasimov JY, Donahue MJ, Gao D, Tu D, Fabiano S. Electropolymerization of Organic Mixed Ionic-Electronic Conductors. Chemical Reviews, 2025, 126(1), 28-79.

conditions: 3,4-Ethylenedioxyselenophene electropolymerizes to films across MeCN and propylene carbonate; oxidation potential ~1.06-1.13 V vs Ag/Ag+ depending on electrolyte; MeCN + TBAClO4 most suitable, multisweep (10-15 cycles, 100 mV/s).
citation: Yadav P, Naqvi S, Patra A. Poly(3,4-ethylenedioxyselenophene): effect of solvent and electrolyte on electrodeposition, optoelectronic and electrochromic properties. RSC Advances, 2020, 10, 12395-12406.

conditions: Polymerizes very readily (rapid electrochemical polymerization, little steric hindrance); oxidation potential ~0.7 V vs SCE in aqueous solution; deposits conductive polypyrrole films (40-100 S/cm).
citation: Gerasimov JY, Donahue MJ, Gao D, Tu D, Fabiano S. Electropolymerization of Organic Mixed Ionic-Electronic Conductors. Chemical Reviews, 2025, 126(1), 28-79.

conditions: Electropolymerizes to black films (e.g. potentiodynamic synthesis at ~0.55 V vs Ag/AgCl in 0.1 M NaDBS aqueous). Both beta positions are blocked so no branching/crosslinking is possible: gives exclusively linear alpha-alpha-linked homopolymer (chains ~1000 units).
citation: Melling D, Wilson SA, Jager EWH. Controlling the electro-mechanical performance of polypyrrole through 3- and 3,4-methyl substituted copolymers. RSC Advances, 2015, 5, 84153-84163.

conditions: Electropolymerizes to poly(3-methylthiophene) films on Pt in acetonitrile / tetrabutylammonium tetrafluoroborate at ~1.5 V; well-established conducting film.
citation: Lapkowski M, et al. A new correlation between the polymerisation charge, film thickness and degree of oxidation of poly(3-methylthiophene). J. Electroanal. Chem., 1998, 449, 89-96 (poly(3-methylthiophene) electrosynthesis in MeCN/Bu4NBF4).

conditions: Electron-donating methoxy lowers monomer oxidation potential to ~1.1 V/SCE (below unsubstituted thiophene); cyclic potential-sweep electropolymerization gives air-stable doped poly(3-methoxythiophene)/oligomer films (perchlorate or dodecyl sulfate doped).
citation: Roncali J and co-workers / micellar-medium electrosynthesis: Electrochemical and spectroscopic properties of poly(3-methoxythiophene) electrosynthesized in an aqueous micellar medium. Synthetic Metals, 1997 (3-methoxythiophene oxidation ~1.1 V/SCE, air-stable doped films).

conditions: Electropolymerizes to polybithiophene films; monomer oxidation potential is lower than that of thiophene or 3-methylthiophene, giving structurally more ordered polythiophene. Acetonitrile commonly used.
citation: Discussed in Gerasimov JY, et al. Electropolymerization of Organic Mixed Ionic-Electronic Conductors. Chemical Reviews, 2025, 126(1), 28-79 (oligomer precursors such as 2,2'-bithiophene reduce the thiophene oxidation potential).

conditions: Electropolymerizes to poly(terthiophene) films; oxidation onset ~0.95 V vs Ag/AgCl-wire (acetonitrile / 0.1 M TBACF3SO3), well below thiophene. Acetonitrile is a suitable solvent; regular alpha,alpha'-linked polymer obtained.
citation: Sheberla D, et al. Conducting polyfurans by electropolymerization of oligofurans. Chemical Science, 2015, 6, 360-371 (Table 1: terthiophene T3 onset 0.95 V vs Ag/AgCl-wire).

conditions: The thiophene-flanked benzothiadiazole monomer (4,7-di(thiophen-2-yl)benzo[c][1,2,5]thiadiazole, DTBT) is anodically oxidized at ~1.5 V vs Ag/AgCl in 0.1 M TBAPF6/dichloromethane (100 mV/s) and electrochemically polymerizes to P(TBT) films. The flanking thiophenes provide the oxidizable, polymerizable sites (electron-poor benzothiadiazole core alone is not the coupling site).
citation: Celik K, Kivrak A, Bezgin Carbas B. A new electrochromic copolymer composed of 4,7-di(thiophen-2-yl)benzo[c][1,2,5]thiadiazole and 3,4-ethylenedioxythiophene. Turkish Journal of Chemistry, 2022, 46(5), 1516-1523.

conditions: Electropolymerizes to poly(3-hexylthiophene) (P3HT) films by oxidative/anodic methods (alkyl-substituted thiophene, polymer oxidation ~1.35 V vs Ag/AgNO3). Demonstrated as an alternative to chemical free-radical/oxidative routes.
citation: Obtaining Poly(3-Hexylthiophene) (P3HT) by Electropolymerization as an Alternative for the Substitution of Initiators with Free Radicals. Polymers, 2025, 17(19), 2656.

conditions: Electropolymerizes readily to conductive polyaniline films from acidic aqueous solution (e.g. 0.5-2 M H2SO4) by cyclic voltammetry / constant potential (~0.6-0.8 V); classic conducting-polymer system. Requires acidic medium for the conducting emeraldine salt.
citation: Electropolymerization of aniline in acid media on bare and chemically pre-treated aluminum electrodes. Electrochimica Acta, 2007 (polyaniline electrodeposition from H2SO4 aqueous; oxidation in 0.1-0.7 V window).

conditions: Electropolymerizes to poly(o-toluidine) but the ortho-methyl group imposes steric (and electronic) effects giving a less planar, shorter, less regular chain with lower doping level and lower conductivity than polyaniline. Film forms but is markedly poorer than PANI.
citation: A comparative study of the effect of organic dopant ions on the electrochemical and chemical synthesis of the conducting polymers polyaniline, poly(o-toluidine) and poly(o-methoxyaniline). Chemical Papers, 2021, 75, 1051-1062.

conditions: Electropolymerizes to poly(o-methoxyaniline) (poly(o-anisidine)) but the ortho-methoxy substituent gives a less regular structure with lower doping level and lower conductivity than polyaniline; films are more soluble/castable. Film forms but conductivity is reduced.
citation: A comparative study of the effect of organic dopant ions on the electrochemical and chemical synthesis of polyaniline, poly(o-toluidine) and poly(o-methoxyaniline). Chemical Papers, 2021, 75, 1051-1062.

conditions: Electropolymerizes to poly(diphenylamine) from acidic aqueous medium (e.g. 2 M H2SO4, ~0.80 V constant potential on ITO); proceeds via 4,4' C-C (benzidine-type) coupling of diphenylamine radical cations. Needs acidic medium.
citation: Studies on poly(diphenylamine) synthesized electrochemically in nonaqueous media. Materials Chemistry and Physics, 1999, 57, 211 (poly(diphenylamine) electrosynthesis; benzidine-type 4,4' coupling).

conditions: Parent 4H-cyclopenta[2,1-b;3,4-b']dithiophene electropolymerizes potentiodynamically to an electroactive film on ITO from 0.1 M LiClO4 in dichloromethane/acetonitrile (1:1 v/v); first-anodic-scan monomer oxidation maximum ~0.85 V; oxidation peak shifts to lower potential on repeated cycling indicating film growth.
citation: E. Tutuncu, B. Varlik, B. Kesimal, A. Cihaner, M. Icli Ozkut, 'An electrochromic polymer based on cyclopenta[2,1-b;3,4-b']dithiophene: Effect of a single atom alteration on the electrochemical and optical properties of the polymer backbone', Synthetic Metals 2020, 265, 116370.

conditions: Electropolymerizes when N-substituted; parent N-H DTP is not the form demonstrated here. N-alkynylated DTP (AlkyneDTP) electropolymerizes to thick adherent dark-blue insoluble films held at +1000 mV vs Ag/AgCl for 150 s in 0.1 M TBAPF6/acetonitrile on ITO or Pt; the click-modified DTPNap analogue likewise films in 0.1 M TBAPF6/CH2Cl2. Studies use N-functionalized DTP rather than the parent N-H heterocycle.
citation: Y. Bandera, H.W. Jones, B. Grant, S. Mell, S.H. Foulger, 'Synthesis, electropolymerization and functionalization via click chemistry of N-alkynylated dithieno[3,2-b:2',3'-d]pyrrole', RSC Advances 2022, 12(45), 29187-29196.

conditions: Thieno[3,4-b]pyrazine (TP) is incorporated as a TP-based terthienyl (flanked by 3-/2-thienyl units) rather than as the bare heterocycle; the terthienyls T1-T5 electropolymerize to films by repeated potential cycling through the anodic peak (100 mV/s) in 0.10 M TBAPF6 in anhydrous CH3CN or CH2Cl2 on Pt disc / ITO (Ag/Ag+ ref). The irreversible terthiophene-backbone oxidation drives radical-cation coupling; bare TP requires the flanking thiophenes to electropolymerize.
citation: K.L. Konkol, R.L. Schwiderski, S.C. Rasmussen, 'Synthesis, Characterization, and Electropolymerization of Extended Fused-Ring Thieno[3,4-b]pyrazine-Based Terthienyls', Materials 2016, 9(6), 404.

conditions: The D-A monomer electropolymerizes only when the 2,1,3-benzothiadiazole acceptor is flanked by thiophene donors. 4,7-Di(thiophen-2-yl)-2,1,3-benzothiadiazole forms a stable low-band-gap film poly(4,7-dithien-2-yl-2,1,3-benzothiadiazole) by oxidative electropolymerization. Bare benzothiadiazole (an electron acceptor) does not homopolymerize oxidatively; the thienyl flank as in the library SMILES is the minimal donor required.
citation: O. Atwani, C. Baristiran, A. Erden, G. Sonmez, 'A stable, low band gap electroactive polymer: Poly(4,7-dithien-2-yl-2,1,3-benzothiadiazole)', Synthetic Metals 2008, 158(3-4), 83-89.

conditions: Corroborating primary source: 4,7-di(thiophen-2-yl)benzo[c][1,2,5]thiadiazole electropolymerizes by cyclic voltammetry (10 cycles, initial oxidation ~1.5 V) in dichloromethane with 0.1 M TBAPF6 on a Pt disc working electrode, Pt wire counter, Ag/AgCl reference; homopolymer band gap 1.32 eV. Requires the flanking thiophenes (acceptor core alone is not oxidatively polymerizable).
citation: K. Celik, A. Kivrak, B. Bezgin Carbas, 'A new electrochromic copolymer composed of 4,7-di(thiophen-2-yl)benzo[c][1,2,5]thiadiazole and 3,4-ethylenedioxythiophene', Turkish Journal of Chemistry 2022, 46(5), 1516-1523.

conditions: Parent furan electropolymerizes only at high potential (ca. 1.8 V vs SCE), where ring opening and irreversible over-oxidation give non-conjugated, low-quality polyfuran. Good conducting films instead require oligofuran precursors (terfuran Fu3 onset 0.62 V; longer oligomers 0.40-0.51 V vs Ag/AgCl wire), which polymerize below ~1.0 V to films of ~1 S/cm.
citation: D. Sheberla, S. Patra, Y.H. Wijsboom, et al., 'Conducting polyfurans by electropolymerization of oligofurans', Chemical Science 2015, 6(1), 360-371.

conditions: Terfuran (Fu3) electropolymerizes at much lower potential than furan monomer (oxidation onset 0.62 V vs Ag/AgCl wire; alkylated/longer oligofurans 0.40-0.51 V), forming stable, smooth (1-5 nm roughness) conducting polyfuran films (~1 S/cm) in acetonitrile-based electrolyte, avoiding the ring-opening/over-oxidation that plagues monomeric furan.
citation: D. Sheberla, S. Patra, Y.H. Wijsboom, et al., 'Conducting polyfurans by electropolymerization of oligofurans', Chemical Science 2015, 6(1), 360-371.

conditions: Freestanding, flexible, homogeneous poly(selenophene) films (conductivity ~1e-1 S/cm) electrosynthesized by direct anodic oxidation of selenophene on a stainless-steel electrode. Note selenophene's oxidation onset is high (~1.83 V vs SCE in acetonitrile/0.1 M Bu4NBF4 per related work); BFEE-type media lower the polymerization potential.
citation: S. Pu, J. Hou, J. Xu, G. Nie, S. Zhang, L. Shen, Q. Xiao, 'Electrosyntheses of freestanding and conducting polyselenophene films', Materials Letters 2005, 59(8-9), 1061-1065.

conditions: EDOT electropolymerizes to PEDOT films; reported at 1.0 V vs Ag/AgNO3 from 0.3 mmol/L EDOT in acetonitrile with 0.1 mol/L TBAP, 5 min deposition. EDOT has a relatively low oxidation potential among thiophenes, enabling mild conditions.
citation: I. Rendon-Enriquez, A. Palma-Cando, F. Korber, et al., 'Thin Polymer Films by Oxidative or Reductive Electropolymerization and Their Application in Electrochromic Windows and Thin-Film Sensors', Molecules 2023, 28(2), 883.

conditions: Bithiophene electropolymerizes to polythiophene-type films from 10 mmol/L in acetonitrile with TBAP, deposited at 0.85 V vs Ag/AgNO3 for 5 min. Lower oxidation potential than thiophene monomer (no high-potential over-oxidation), so commonly used to grow polythiophene films.
citation: I. Rendon-Enriquez, A. Palma-Cando, F. Korber, et al., 'Thin Polymer Films by Oxidative or Reductive Electropolymerization and Their Application in Electrochromic Windows and Thin-Film Sensors', Molecules 2023, 28(2), 883.

conditions: Pyrrole electropolymerizes to polypyrrole films under mild conditions; reported at 0.6 V vs Ag/AgCl from 50 mmol/L pyrrole in aqueous buffer (pH 3, NaNO3/HNO3), 5 min. Low monomer oxidation potential allows aqueous and organic deposition.
citation: I. Rendon-Enriquez, A. Palma-Cando, F. Korber, et al., 'Thin Polymer Films by Oxidative or Reductive Electropolymerization and Their Application in Electrochromic Windows and Thin-Film Sensors', Molecules 2023, 28(2), 883.

conditions: Aniline electropolymerizes to polyaniline (emeraldine) films, but conditionally on acidic medium: reported at 0.7 V vs Ag/AgCl from 400 mmol/L aniline in water with H2SO4, 5 min. Conductive emeraldine salt forms only in strongly acidic media (e.g., HCl/H2SO4).
citation: I. Rendon-Enriquez, A. Palma-Cando, F. Korber, et al., 'Thin Polymer Films by Oxidative or Reductive Electropolymerization and Their Application in Electrochromic Windows and Thin-Film Sensors', Molecules 2023, 28(2), 883.

conditions: Carbazole oxidizes to a reactive radical cation that couples at the 3,6-positions; with both 3,6-positions free the coupling can terminate at the 3,3'-bicarbazyl dimer rather than a film, whereas designs that keep reactive 3,6-coupling sites available (e.g., 3,6-bis(N-carbazolyl) carbazoles) readily electropolymerize to polycarbazole films via oxidative coupling, the dimer/oligomer having lower oxidation potential than monomer to sustain film growth.
citation: 'Electrochemical synthesis of electrochromic polycarbazole films from N-phenyl-3,6-bis(N-carbazolyl)carbazoles', Polymer Chemistry (RSC) 2016, DOI 10.1039/C5PY01407G.

conditions: Anodic oxidation in acetonitrile or aqueous media with R4NPF6/R4NBF4 or LiClO4; EDOT oxidizes at low potential (Eox ~1.4 V vs SCE/Ag) giving the very stable, conductive electrochromic film PEDOT. Benchmark 'easy' electropolymerizing monomer.
citation: Groenendaal, L.; Jonas, F.; Freitag, D.; Pielartzik, H.; Reynolds, J. R. Poly(3,4-ethylenedioxythiophene) and Its Derivatives: Past, Present, and Future. Advanced Materials 2000, 12 (7), 481-494.

conditions: Forms polythiophene by anodic oxidation but only at high potential (monomer onset ~1.8-2.0 V vs SCE in neutral ACN), where overoxidation/degradation degrades film quality; lower potentials give only oligomers. Practical films favored in BFEE or from oligomers. Classified conditional because plain-ACN films are poor unless potential is carefully controlled.
citation: Sheberla, D.; Patra, S.; Wijsboom, Y. H.; Sharma, S.; Sheynin, Y.; Haj-Yahia, A.-E.; Barak, A. H.; Gidron, O.; Bendikov, M. Conducting polyfurans by electropolymerization of oligofurans. Chemical Science 2015, 6 (1), 360-371.

conditions: Anodic oxidation in acetonitrile / Bu4NBF4; electron-donating methyl lowers monomer oxidation to ~1.5 V vs SCE (about 0.2 V below unsubstituted thiophene), giving good poly(3-methylthiophene) films.
citation: Roncali, J.; Garreau, R.; Yassar, A.; Marque, P.; Garnier, F.; Lemaire, M. Effects of Steric Factors on the Electrosynthesis and Properties of Conducting Poly(3-alkylthiophenes). J. Phys. Chem. 1987, 91 (27), 6706-6714.

conditions: Anodic oxidation in acetonitrile; 2,2'-bithiophene oxidizes at lower potential than thiophene (monomer oxidation onset ~0.81 V), giving polythiophene films with less overoxidation than from the monomer.
citation: Krische, B.; Zagorska, M. Polythiophene synthesis by electropolymerization of thiophene and bithiophene. Synthetic Metals 1989, 33 (3), 257-267.

conditions: Anodic oxidation in ACN (TBA salt); low monomer onset (~0.95 V vs SCE; oxidation peak ~0.65-1.07 V), giving regular alpha,alpha'-linked polyterthiophene films with better cyclability than poly(thiophene)/poly(bithiophene). Potential >0.8 V introduces mislinkage; <0.7 V gives only oligomers.
citation: Sheberla, D.; et al. (incl. Gidron, O.; Bendikov, M.). Conducting polyfurans by electropolymerization of oligofurans. Chemical Science 2015, 6 (1), 360-371 (Table 1: terthiophene onset 0.95 V vs SCE).

conditions: Furan monomer requires very high potential (~1.8 V vs SCE) where ring opening and irreversible degradation dominate, so plain-solvent films are poor. Good free-standing polyfuran films require either a Lewis-acid (BFEE) medium or starting from oligofurans (bi-/terfuran), which oxidize <1.0 V.
citation: Sheberla, D.; Patra, S.; Wijsboom, Y. H.; Sharma, S.; Sheynin, Y.; Haj-Yahia, A.-E.; Barak, A. H.; Gidron, O.; Bendikov, M. Conducting polyfurans by electropolymerization of oligofurans. Chemical Science 2015, 6 (1), 360-371.

conditions: Anodic oxidation in acetonitrile / 0.1 M TBACF3SO3; 2,2'-bifuran onset oxidation ~0.8 V (vs ~1.25/1.8 V for furan), polymerizing below 1.0 V to give good conducting polyfuran films, avoiding the ring-opening degradation seen with furan.
citation: Sheberla, D.; Patra, S.; Wijsboom, Y. H.; Sharma, S.; Sheynin, Y.; Haj-Yahia, A.-E.; Barak, A. H.; Gidron, O.; Bendikov, M. Conducting polyfurans by electropolymerization of oligofurans. Chemical Science 2015, 6 (1), 360-371.

conditions: Anodic oxidation in acetonitrile / 0.1 M TBACF3SO3; terfuran onset oxidation 0.62 V vs SCE (below terthiophene 0.95 V), polymerizing readily below 1.0 V to give smooth, stable conducting polyfuran films (conductivity ~1 S/cm).
citation: Sheberla, D.; Patra, S.; Wijsboom, Y. H.; Sharma, S.; Sheynin, Y.; Haj-Yahia, A.-E.; Barak, A. H.; Gidron, O.; Bendikov, M. Conducting polyfurans by electropolymerization of oligofurans. Chemical Science 2015, 6 (1), 360-371.

conditions: High-quality freestanding polyselenophene films deposited from neat boron trifluoride diethyl etherate (BFEE) + 20 mM selenophene, where oxidation potential is much lower than in ACN/Bu4NBF4 and below thiophene in BFEE; conductivity up to 0.28 S/cm. In plain ACN the oxidation potential is high and films are poorer, so the BFEE/Lewis-acid medium is the enabling condition.
citation: Li, C.; Shi, G.; Liang, Y.; et al. Electrosyntheses of freestanding and conducting polyselenophene films. Materials Letters 2005, 59 (8-9), 1163-1166.

conditions: Anodic oxidation in aqueous or organic media; low onset (~0.9 V vs NHE in water), readily forms adherent conducting polypyrrole films. Benchmark 'easy' monomer; Diaz mechanism (alpha-coupling of radical cations).
citation: Asavapiriyanont, S.; Chandler, G. K.; Gunawardena, G. A.; Pletcher, D. The electrodeposition of polypyrrole films from aqueous solutions. J. Electroanal. Chem. (related Pletcher series); see also Diaz, A. F. et al. Electrochemical polymerization of pyrrole in aqueous solutions, Synthetic Metals 1987, 21, 247-253.

conditions: Anodic oxidation in acetonitrile (also aqueous); forms poly(N-methylpyrrole) films, but conductivity is markedly lower than polypyrrole because N-methylation blocks N-coupling and twists the backbone. Film forms but is more resistive/lower quality.
citation: Influence of electrochemical process parameters on the conductivity of poly(N-methylpyrrole) films by galvanostatic method. Materials Letters 2006 (and refs therein on poly-N-methylpyrrole, ScienceDirect S0167577X05011614).

conditions: Anodic oxidation in 0.1 M aqueous NaDBS at 0.55 V vs Ag/AgCl gives black conducting poly(3,4-dimethylpyrrole) films. 3,4-Dimethyl blocks beta-positions so only alpha-coupling occurs, reducing crosslinking/branching and improving order vs polypyrrole.
citation: Melling, D.; Wilson, S. A.; Jager, E. W. H. Controlling the electro-mechanical performance of polypyrrole through 3- and 3,4-methyl substituted copolymers. RSC Advances 2015, 5, 84153-84163.

conditions: Anodic oxidation in ACN (e.g., LiClO4 or TBA salt); very low oxidation potential due to dialkoxy donor on pyrrole, giving the highly stable electrochromic conducting polymer PEDOP. Pyrrole analogue of PEDOT.
citation: Schottland, P.; Zong, K.; Gaupp, C. L.; Thompson, B. C.; Thomas, C. A.; Giurgiu, I.; Hickman, R.; Abboud, K. A.; Reynolds, J. R. Poly(3,4-alkylenedioxypyrrole)s: Highly Stable Electronically Conducting and Electrochromic Polymers. Macromolecules 2000, 33 (19), 7051-7061.

conditions: Anodic oxidation in acetonitrile / 0.1 M LiClO4 (~1e-2 M monomer, chronoamperometry) gives thick green polycarbazole films; coupling at 3,6-positions. Unsubstituted carbazole deposits well.
citation: Contal, E.; Sougueh, C. M.; Lakard, S.; Et Taouil, A.; Magnenet, C.; Lakard, B. Investigation of Polycarbazoles Thin Films Prepared by Electrochemical Oxidation of Synthesized Carbazole Derivatives. Frontiers in Materials 2019, 6, 131.

conditions: Anodic oxidation on Pt in acetonitrile with various supporting salts; forms poly(N-vinylcarbazole) films via carbazole 3,3'(/3,6) coupling plus H+-initiated crosslinking through the vinyl groups; thick uniform green films.
citation: Contal, E.; Sougueh, C. M.; Lakard, S.; Et Taouil, A.; Magnenet, C.; Lakard, B. Investigation of Polycarbazoles Thin Films Prepared by Electrochemical Oxidation of Synthesized Carbazole Derivatives. Frontiers in Materials 2019, 6, 131 (CzV forms thick uniform green films).

conditions: Fluorene and 9,9-dialkylfluorenes electropolymerize by 2,7-coupling, but in ACN the oxidation potential is high and coupling is sluggish (dimer radical cation poorly reactive); good polyfluorene films obtained in BFEE (fluorene Eox ~1.1 V vs SCE, conductivity ~0.25 S/cm). Film forms readily only under the activating BFEE/Lewis-acid medium.
citation: Low-potential electrochemical polymerization of fluorene and its alkyl-polymer precursor. Electrochimica Acta 2006 (ScienceDirect S0013468606000508).

conditions: As for 9,9-dialkylfluorenes: oxidative 2,7-coupling gives polyfluorene films, but the dimer radical cation is weakly reactive in ACN; reliable films require BFEE/Lewis-acid medium or pre-formed dimer/trimer precursors. 9,9-disubstitution prevents 9-position side reactions.
citation: Comparative Study of the Oxidation of Fluorene and 9,9-Disubstituted Fluorenes and Their Related 2,7'-Dimers and Trimer. Chemistry of Materials 2005 (ACS, doi 10.1021/cm048331o).

conditions: Electropolymerizes to conducting/electroactive polyaniline only from acidic aqueous media (pH < 3); the redox-active conducting emeraldine salt requires acid. Films grown at pH > 3 are essentially non-conducting/degraded. Acidic medium is the required condition.
citation: Sazou, D.; et al. Electropolymerization of aniline in aqueous solutions at pH 2 to 12. Journal of Materials Science 2005, 40, ... (Springer, doi 10.1007/s10853-005-0854-x); polyaniline conducting only for pH < 3.

conditions: Anodic oxidation in acidic aqueous media (e.g., H2SO4 or sulfonic acids); forms electroactive poly(o-anisidine/o-methoxyaniline) films (e.g., 90% yield, ~0.1 S/cm with p-TSA). Like aniline, requires acidic medium for conducting film.
citation: Synthesis and characterization of poly(o-anisidine) films. Synthetic Metals 1992 (ScienceDirect 0379677992903053).

conditions: Anodic oxidation in acidic aqueous media (e.g., 1 M H2SO4, potential cycling -0.2 to 1.0 V vs Ag/AgCl); forms poly(o-toluidine) films, but lower conductivity than polyaniline due to steric/electronic effect of the ortho-methyl. Acidic medium required.
citation: Electrochemical and optical behaviour of conducting polymer: poly(o-toluidine). European Polymer Journal 1999 (ScienceDirect S0014305798001785).

conditions: Electropolymerizes to poly(diphenylamine) films in acidic aqueous or organic media; proceeds by 4,4'-C-C (phenyl-phenyl) coupling of radical cations (not N-coupling), giving a PANI/poly(p-phenylene)-hybrid structure. Acidic/organic medium and the para coupling pathway are the special conditions.
citation: Comninellis, C.; et al. Formation of poly(4-phenylaniline) by electropolymerization of 4-aminobiphenyl or diphenylamine. J. Electroanal. Chem. 1989 (ScienceDirect 0022072889870354).

conditions: Anodic oxidation in acetonitrile / 0.1 M TBAPF6; DTP has a very low oxidation onset (~0.52-0.77 V vs Ag/AgCl). Electropolymerizing at ~+1.0 V gives thick adherent dark-blue, insoluble, electroactive/electrochromic films.
citation: Bandera, Y.; Jones, H. W.; Grant, B.; Mell, S.; Foulger, S. H. Synthesis, electropolymerization and functionalization via click chemistry of N-alkynylated dithieno[3,2-b:2',3'-d]pyrrole. RSC Advances 2022, 12 (45), 29187-29196.

conditions: Anodic oxidation (potentiodynamic) on glassy carbon or ITO in ACN; the fused, electron-rich cyclopentadithiophene oxidizes readily and forms electroactive/electrochromic poly(CPDT) films (alpha-coupling of the terminal thiophenes).
citation: An electrochromic polymer based on cyclopenta[2,1-b;3,4-b']dithiophene... Synthetic Metals 2020 (ScienceDirect S0379677920301284).

conditions: Anodic oxidation; the methoxy donor lowers monomer oxidation potential below thiophene, giving poly(3-methoxythiophene) films. Electrosynthesized in aqueous micellar medium and in ionic liquids (oxidized at lower potential than EDOT in hydrophilic IL).
citation: Electrochemical and spectroscopic properties of poly(3-methoxythiophene) electrosynthesized in an aqueous micellar medium. Synthetic Metals 1997 (ScienceDirect S0379677997041039).

conditions: Strongly electron-withdrawing fluorines make the 3,4-difluorothiophene ring electron-poor, raising the oxidation potential and making direct anodic electropolymerization difficult; practical polymerization uses the activated 2,5-bis(trimethylsilyl)-3,4-difluorothiophene (TMS as cationic leaving group) rather than the parent monomer.
citation: Process for preparing n-doping conjugated electroactive polymer using poly(3,4-difluorothiophene). US Patent 7,399,433 (BTMSDFT monomer; difluorothiophene is electron-poor and harder to oxidize than typical thiophenes).
